definition: Positive-to-negative correlation transitions are fast (median ~3 months from crystallizing event) while negative-to-positive transitions unfold gradually (median ~12 months), a 4:1 asymmetry robust across all 13 post-1960 transitions.
evidence: 8 positive-to-negative transitions averaged ~3 months (Penn Central 1970, LTCM 1998, Lehman 2008)
5 negative-to-positive transitions averaged ~12 months through gradual parameter drift
VIX differential corroborates: average 30-45 during negative snaps vs. 18-25 during positive drift
Labeled 'single most actionable historical finding' in pair_0 debate
open_questions: Does the asymmetry hold when fiscal deficits are conditioning the transition?
What is the portfolio construction implication for pre-positioning?
